message:
test(core): property-based tests for token bucket rate limiter (wa-fsep)

22 proptest properties verifying:
- Tokens never exceed capacity after any operation
- Monotonic total_consumed and total_denied
- Successful acquire increments consumed by exact cost
- Refill correctness: rate * elapsed, capped at capacity
- No refill on backward/same timestamps
- Wait time: zero when available, succeeds after wait, monotonic in cost
- Reset restores full capacity
- Stats: fill_ratio in [0,1], BucketStats/BucketConfig serde roundtrip
- Config build: start_empty vs start_full behavior
- Hierarchical atomicity: denied ops consume from neither bucket
- Hierarchical bottleneck identification: DeniedLocal vs DeniedGlobal
- Dynamic rate change takes effect on next refill
- try_acquire_one equivalence with try_acquire(1, ...)
- Token conservation: avail + consumed <= initial + refilled
- Non-negative tokens throughout any operation sequence

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-090v","title":"[EPIC] Safety & policy engine (capability gates, rate limits, redaction)","description":"# Safety & policy engine

## Goal
Ensure wa never becomes a liability by enforcing deterministic safety constraints before any action that mutates state.

## Why this is critical
We are automating typing into terminals running powerful tools. A single wrong send can cause data loss.
We therefore treat action authorization as a first-class subsystem.

## Design requirements
- **Capability-based gates** (deterministic):
  - never send into alt-screen
  - require prompt-active for SendText where possible (OSC 133)
  - block SendText if output gap indicates uncertain state
- **Rate limiting** per pane and per action kind
- **Approval modes**:
  - interactive: prompt user for approvals
  - robot/mcp: return `RequireApproval` as an error with a clear reason
- **Secret redaction** for audit logs and outputs

## Deliverables
- `ActionKind` classification (SendText/Spawn/Split/BrowserAuth/WriteFile/ExecuteWorkflow/etc.)
- `PolicyEngine` evaluation:
  - allow/deny/require-approval
  - rule table loaded from config
- `Redactor`:
  - recognize API key patterns, tokens, passwords
  - ensure secrets never appear in logs or robot output
- Integration points:
  - all sends and workflows must call policy authorize

## Acceptance
- Attempting SendText while pane is AltScreen or CommandRunning is denied.
- Policy logs include redacted input.
- Robot mode returns stable error codes for denied actions.



## Success Criteria
- All input injection paths are policy-gated (robot/human/workflow) with safe defaults.
- Policy decisions are explainable (stable reasons + rule ids) and auditable (including denials).
- Secret redaction is applied consistently to logs/audit/export artifacts.
- Approval (allow-once) works with tight scoping and TTL, and is audited.
- Unit/integration tests cover capability gates, rate limiting, redaction, and approval behavior; E2E covers deny/approve flows.


## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - Capability gate matrix: {PromptActive, CommandRunning, AltScreen, RecentGap} × action kinds → expected decision.
  - Approval allow-once scoping (command hash, TTL) and audit output stability.
  - Redaction property tests: secrets never appear in structured logs, audit records, or exported artifacts.

- Integration tests:
  - Wire policy into robot send + workflow send paths and assert:
    - deny returns stable error codes
    - deny/approve decisions are recorded in audit storage

- E2E tests:
  - Deny-by-default scenario for unsafe sends + explicit allow-once override with artifacts (`wa-4vx.10.10`, `wa-4vx.10.16`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-0d0k","title":"CLI command timeout hardening — kill_on_drop and orphan reaper","description":"# CLI Command Timeout Hardening — kill_on_drop and Orphan Reaper

## Goal
Add aggressive timeout enforcement and orphan process cleanup to wa CLI subprocess spawning, preventing stuck wezterm cli process accumulation.

## Background
WezTerm CLI commands can hang indefinitely due to lock contention, no socket read timeout, notification feedback loops. We observed 60+ stuck processes on production servers.

## kill_on_drop Wrapper
Modify run_cli() in wezterm.rs:
- tokio::Command with kill_on_drop(true)
- Enforce configurable timeout (default 15s)
- On timeout: child is dropped → killed → Error::CliTimeout returned

## Orphan Reaper
Periodic background task in wa watch:
- Scan for wezterm cli processes older than threshold (default 30s)
- **Linux**: pgrep + /proc/<pid>/stat for process age
- **macOS**: pgrep + ps -o etime= for process age
- Kill orphans with SIGKILL after age threshold
- Log reap report: scanned count, killed count

## Configuration
```toml
[cli]
timeout_seconds = 15
orphan_reap_interval = 60
orphan_age_seconds = 30
```

## Tests
- CLI command respects timeout (mock slow command)
- kill_on_drop cleanly terminates timed-out processes
- Orphan reaper identifies and kills old processes
- Reap report logged with correct counts
- No FD or resource leaks from killed processes
- **Test on both Linux and macOS** (process age detection differs)
- **Criterion benchmarks**: reaper scan <10ms for 1000 processes

## Acceptance criteria
- CLI commands timeout after configured seconds
- kill_on_drop ensures cleanup even on wa crash/panic
- Orphan reaper runs periodically
- Works correctly on Linux and macOS
- No resource leaks from killed processes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"RusticSnow","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:19:32.031847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.301267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:52:35.472539-05:00","close_reason":"CLI timeout hardening complete: kill_on_drop(true) on Command in run_cli(), orphan_reaper module with single-ps scan, CliConfig in config.rs, wired into watcher","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-0d0k","depends_on_id":"wa-3cyp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:37:29.069976Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"wa-0fpd","title":"Persist ActionPlans + step logs; expose via CLI/robot for explainability","description":"# Task: Persist ActionPlans and step logs

## Goal
Make ActionPlans and per-step execution logs durable and queryable for explainability, debugging, and replay.

## Requirements
- Storage:
  - persist ActionPlan (canonical JSON + hash) linked to workflow execution
  - persist step logs with:
    - step id/kind
    - policy decision summary
    - verification evidence references
    - errors with stable codes
- Query surfaces:
  - robot/MCP: fetch plan and step logs by workflow id
  - human CLI: view plan/logs with progressive disclosure

## Testing
- Storage integration tests:
  - insert plan + step logs and query roundtrip
  - schema migration stability

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a workflow execution id, wa can show:
  - the ActionPlan that was executed
  - the step-by-step results and evidence

 {"id":"wa-0go","title":"[EPIC] Natural Language Event Descriptions: human-readable event summaries","description":"# [EPIC] Natural Language Event Descriptions

## Mission
Transform cryptic event types and codes into clear, human-readable descriptions that users immediately understand.

## Why This Matters
Current events are technical:
- `codex.usage_limit_reached` - What does this mean?
- `WA-4040` - What happened?

Natural language transforms:
- "Codex hit your daily usage limit. You can switch accounts or wait until reset."
- "The send was blocked because the pane is in alternate screen mode (vim is running)."

## Scope

### Event Templates
Each event type has a human-readable template:
```rust
pub struct EventTemplate {
    event_type: String,
    summary: String,           // "Codex usage limit reached"
    description: String,       // Full explanation
    context_keys: Vec<String>, // Variables to interpolate
    suggestions: Vec<String>,  // What user can do
}
```

### Template Interpolation
```
Event: codex.usage_limit_reached
Summary: "Codex reached 100% of daily usage limit"
Description: "Your Codex session in pane {pane_title} has consumed 
             all available tokens for today. The session will pause 
             until {reset_time}."
Suggestions:
  - "Switch to a different account with `wa accounts switch`"
  - "Wait for reset at {reset_time}"
  - "Check usage details with `wa analytics`"
```

### Error Code Descriptions
```rust
pub fn describe_error(code: &str) -> String {
    match code {
        "WA-4040" => "The specified pane was not found. It may have been closed.",
        "WA-4030" => "This action was blocked by policy. The pane may be in a 
                     protected state (running command, alternate screen, etc.)",
        // ...
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
- `wa events` shows natural language summaries
- `wa why <error>` explains decisions
- Error messages include descriptions

## Success Criteria
- Users understand events without looking up codes
- Descriptions are actionable
- All events have human-readable descriptions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All event types have templates
- [ ] All error codes have descriptions
- [ ] Interpolation works correctly
- [ ] Suggestions are relevant
- [ ] CLI shows natural language output
- [ ] Tests cover all templates

## Testing
- Unit tests for template coverage, interpolation, and redaction.
- Integration tests for CLI rendering.
- E2E artifacts include rendered summaries.

 {"id":"wa-0go.1","title":"Event description templates + interpolation engine","description":"# Task: Event description templates + interpolation engine

## Goal
Create a **central, versioned registry** of human‑readable descriptions for every event type and error code.

## Scope
- Event templates (summary + description + suggestions)
- Error code descriptions (e.g., WA-4xxx)
- Template interpolation with **strict redaction**
- Stable identifiers for templates so diffs are auditable

## Deliverables
1. **Template registry** (static data + loader):
   - `event_type -> template`
   - `error_code -> description`
2. **Interpolation engine**:
   - required keys validated at runtime
   - missing keys produce structured “incomplete context” errors
   - redaction pass applied before rendering
3. **Schema/format**:
   - docstring or JSON schema for templates
   - version number for template registry

## Safety / Redaction
- Run redaction before interpolation output is surfaced.
- Never emit raw tokens, device codes, or secret‑like strings.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - template coverage: every event_type has a template
  - interpolation: required keys enforced, missing keys yield structured errors
  - redaction: known secret patterns are removed from rendered output

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every event type and error code has a template entry.
- Interpolation failures are explicit and non‑fatal.
- Redaction is applied consistently to all rendered strings.

 {"id":"wa-fno.4","title":"Extension sandboxing: WASM or Lua runtime for safe custom code execution","description":"
# Extension Sandboxing

## Purpose
Run custom extension code safely without risking system security.

## Options Evaluated

### 1. WASM (Recommended)
- Pros: Strong isolation, multi-language, proven
- Cons: Larger binary, more complex
- Libraries: wasmtime, wasmer

### 2. Lua
- Pros: Simple, fast, battle-tested (Neovim/WezTerm)
- Cons: Single language, needs careful sandboxing
- Libraries: rlua, mlua

## Recommendation: WASM via wasmtime
- Better isolation guarantees
- Supports Rust, Go, C, AssemblyScript
- WASI for controlled system access

## Sandbox Capabilities
```rust
pub struct ExtensionSandbox {
    engine: wasmtime::Engine,
    linker: wasmtime::Linker<SandboxState>,
    capabilities: SandboxCapabilities,
}

pub struct SandboxCapabilities {
    pub read_pane_output: bool,
    pub send_notifications: bool,
    pub http_requests: bool,
    pub file_access: FileAccessScope,
}
```

## Capability Levels
- Level 1: Read-only (patterns only)
- Level 2: Read + notify (most workflows)
- Level 3: Read + notify + HTTP (integrations)
- Level 4: Full (admin extensions)

## Extension Manifest
```toml
[extension]
name = "my-patterns"
capabilities = ["read_pane_output"]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WASM runtime integrated
- [ ] Capability-based permissions
- [ ] Extensions cannot access system without permission
- [ ] Untrusted extension cannot crash wa

## Testing
- Unit tests for sandbox policy enforcement and resource limits.
- Integration tests running a restricted extension in a sandbox.
- Security checks: verify no filesystem/network escape.

 {"id":"wa-fno.5","title":"Extension tests: loading, lifecycle, sandbox isolation, security","description":"
# Extension Testing Suite

## Purpose
Ensure extension system is reliable and secure.

## Test Categories

### 1. Loading Tests
- Pattern packs load from user directory
- Workflow definitions parse correctly
- Invalid extensions fail gracefully
- Conflicting extensions handled

### 2. Lifecycle Tests
- Extension install works
- Extension list shows installed
- Extension remove works
- Extension update works

### 3. Sandbox Tests
- Extensions cannot access filesystem beyond scope
- Extensions cannot make network calls without permission
- Extensions cannot crash host process
- Extensions terminated on timeout

### 4. Security Tests
- Malicious extension cannot escape sandbox
- Resource limits enforced (memory, CPU)
- Capability checks enforced
- No privilege escalation

### 5. Integration Tests
- Custom patterns detected correctly
- Custom workflows execute correctly
- Custom notification channels deliver

## Test Extensions
- Benign test extension for happy path
- Malicious test extension for security testing
- Resource-hungry extension for limits testing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% coverage for extension loading
- [ ] Sandbox escape attempts blocked
- [ ] Resource limits enforced
- [ ] Security tests pass
- [ ] Documentation tests (examples work)

## Testing
- Extension lifecycle tests (load, run, unload) with isolation guarantees.
- Negative tests for sandbox escape attempts.
- E2E: run a sample extension with verbose logs.

 {"id":"wa-fpqc","title":"Implement incident bundle collector (manifest + redaction + bounded logs)","description":"# Task: Implement incident bundle collector

## Goal
Implement the core bundle builder that gathers, sanitizes, and packages incident artifacts according to the spec.

## Requirements
- Implement bundle directory layout and `manifest.json` versioning.
- Gather artifacts (all bounded by privacy budget):
  - watcher logs (structured logs with correlation IDs)
  - health snapshot (queue depth/lag)
  - recent events/detections and policy decisions (sanitized)
  - DB metadata: schema version, size, WAL mode, last checkpoint
  - minimal DB slice export (optional; safe copy + WAL handling)
- Redaction:
  - reuse central redaction logic used by audit/export
  - produce `redaction_report.json` with counts and rule IDs
- Determinism:
  - stable ordering in manifests and file lists
  - stable timestamps in manifest: include both wall clock and monotonic-derived durations when available

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - privacy budget enforcement (truncate behavior deterministic)
  - redaction: secrets never appear
  - manifest determinism across runs
- Integration tests:
  - build bundle from fixture DB/logs and assert required files exist

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundles are created successfully with bounded size.
- Bundles contain no secrets by default.
- Bundle structure matches the spec and is deterministic.

 {"id":"wa-fwvx","title":"Explanation templates: reusable reason patterns for wa why and errors","description":"# Explanation Templates: Reusable reason patterns for wa why

## Purpose
Provide consistent, helpful explanations for common scenarios via template system.

## Implementation

### Template Structure
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ExplanationTemplate {
    pub id: &'static str,
    pub scenario: &'static str,
    pub brief: &'static str,
    pub detailed: &'static str,
    pub suggestions: Vec<&'static str>,
    pub see_also: Vec<&'static str>,
}
```

### Built-in Templates

#### Policy Denials
```rust
pub static DENY_ALT_SCREEN: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "deny.alt_screen",
    scenario: "Send denied because alt-screen is active",
    brief: "Pane is in full-screen mode (vim, less, etc.)",
    detailed: r#"
The pane is currently displaying an alternate screen buffer, which typically
means a full-screen application like vim, less, htop, or similar is running.

Sending text while alt-screen is active could:
- Corrupt the application state
- Cause unintended keystrokes
- Interfere with user interaction

The safety policy blocks sends to alt-screen panes by default.
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Exit the full-screen application first",
        "Use --force if you're certain this is safe",
        "Configure policy to allow specific alt-screen apps",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa policy", "wa status --pane <id>"],
};

pub static DENY_COMMAND_RUNNING: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "deny.command_running",
    scenario: "Send denied because a command is running",
    brief: "Another command is currently executing in the pane",
    detailed: r#"
The pane has an active command running (detected via OSC 133 markers or
heuristics). Sending text while a command runs could:

- Interrupt the running command
- Queue input for later (confusing)
- Cause the shell to misinterpret input

wa waits for command completion before sending unless overridden.
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Wait for the current command to finish",
        "Use Ctrl-C to cancel the running command first",
        "Use --wait-for to send after a specific pattern",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa status", "wa send --wait-for"],
};

pub static DENY_RECENT_GAP: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "deny.recent_gap",
    scenario: "Send denied due to recent output gap",
    brief: "Pane had no output recently, possibly waiting for input",
    detailed: r#"
wa detected a gap in pane output that suggests the pane might be:
- Waiting for user input at a prompt
- Displaying a confirmation dialog
- In an unknown state

The policy requires a prompt marker (OSC 133) or manual confirmation.
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Check the pane manually to see its state",
        "Use --force if you've verified the pane is ready",
        "Enable OSC 133 support in your shell for better detection",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa capabilities --pane <id>"],
};
```

#### Workflow Explanations
```rust
pub static WORKFLOW_USAGE_LIMIT: ExplanationTemplate = ExplanationTemplate {
    id: "workflow.usage_limit",
    scenario: "Why handle_usage_limits workflow was triggered",
    brief: "Codex hit its daily token usage limit",
    detailed: r#"
The Codex agent reported it has reached its usage limit. This typically
happens when:

- Daily token quota exceeded
- Account-level rate limiting triggered

The handle_usage_limits workflow will:
1. Gracefully exit the current Codex session
2. Parse the session summary for resume ID
3. Select an alternate OpenAI account
4. Complete device auth flow
5. Resume the session with new credentials
"#,
    suggestions: vec![
        "Let the workflow complete automatically",
        "Check account status with: caut status",
        "Configure account pool in wa.toml",
    ],
    see_also: vec!["wa workflow status", "caut"],
};
```

### Template Registry
```rust
lazy_static! {
    pub static ref EXPLANATION_TEMPLATES: HashMap<&'static str, &'static ExplanationTemplate> = {
        let mut m = HashMap::new();
        m.insert("deny.alt_screen", &DENY_ALT_SCREEN);
        m.insert("deny.command_running", &DENY_COMMAND_RUNNING);
        m.insert("deny.recent_gap", &DENY_RECENT_GAP);
        m.insert("workflow.usage_limit", &WORKFLOW_USAGE_LIMIT);
        // ... more templates
        m
    };
}

pub fn get_explanation(id: &str) -> Option<&'static ExplanationTemplate> {
    EXPLANATION_TEMPLATES.get(id).copied()
}
```

### Template Interpolation
```rust
pub fn render_explanation(
    template: &ExplanationTemplate,
    context: &HashMap<String, String>,
) -> String {
    let mut output = template.detailed.to_string();
    for (key, value) in context {
        output = output.replace(&format!("{{{}}}", key), value);
    }
    output
}
```

## Testing
- All templates have valid structure
- Interpolation works correctly
- No broken cross-references

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Templates for all common denial reasons
- [ ] Templates for all workflow triggers
- [ ] wa why uses templates consistently
- [ ] Templates documented in help

 {"id":"wa-fykv","title":"FTUI-03.2.a Route in-process logs through single output sink","description":"## Background
In-process output must obey one-writer constraints to keep rendering deterministic and avoid output corruption.

## Deliverables
- inventory and removal plan for direct stdout/stderr writes in UI-active paths
- unified output sink routing contract for logs/events/status lines
- verification checks to prove no bypass writes remain

## Acceptance Criteria
- all targeted paths route through the sanctioned sink while UI is active
- checks detect and fail on new bypass writes
- validation evidence includes unit assertions and runtime logs demonstrating routing correctness.,","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:05.996650629Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.260744-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:12:15.49917289Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-fykv","depends_on_id":"wa-m76j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-g08v","title":"Quick-fix tests: template interpolation, error remediation coverage, UX validation","description":"
# Quick-Fix Testing Suite

## Purpose
Ensure all suggestions are accurate, copy-pasteable, and helpful.

## Test Categories

### 1. Template Interpolation Tests
- {pane} substitution works
- {event_id} substitution works
- Invalid templates fail gracefully

### 2. Error Remediation Coverage
- Every WaError variant has remediation
- Remediations are non-empty
- Commands are syntactically valid

### 3. UX Validation Tests
- Commands can be copy-pasted (no smart quotes, etc.)
- Commands are actually runnable
- Suggestions match actual system state

### 4. Context-Aware Tests
- Typo detection finds correct suggestions
- Available resources are accurate
- Platform detection is correct

### 5. Snapshot Tests
- Error message format stability
- Suggestion format stability

## Test Fixtures
- Error scenarios with known remediations
- Event scenarios with known suggestions
- Platform-specific command sets

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% of error types have remediation tests
- [ ] 100% of rule types have suggestion tests
- [ ] Snapshot tests for output stability
- [ ] Copy-paste validation (no encoding issues)

## Testing
- Unit tests for template rendering and suggested_action interpolation.
- Integration tests: fixture errors/events produce actionable suggestions.
- E2E: extend an events/error scenario to assert suggestions and rule-id logs.
